result:
All from direct file reads (repo root: `/Users/swchen.tw/kernel-bench/work-codegraph`), line numbers verified.

## (a) Kconfig option enabling e1000e

`drivers/net/ethernet/intel/Kconfig:58-76`
```
58: config E1000E
59:     tristate "Intel(R) PRO/1000 PCI-Express Gigabit Ethernet support"
60:     depends on PCI && (!SPARC32 || BROKEN)
61:     depends on PTP_1588_CLOCK_OPTIONAL
62:     select CRC32
```
(also `config E1000E_HWTS` at line 78, `depends on E1000E && X86`, bool default y â€” optional HW cross-timestamp sub-feature, not required for the base driver.)

## (b) Makefile rule compiling e1000e objects

Driver-local `drivers/net/ethernet/intel/e1000e/Makefile`:
```
11: obj-$(CONFIG_E1000E) += e1000e.o
13: e1000e-objs := 82571.o ich8lan.o 80003es2lan.o \
14:                mac.o manage.o nvm.o phy.o \
15:                param.o ethtool.o netdev.o ptp.o
```

## (c) Full build-gating chain (Makefile side + Kconfig side)

**Makefile descent (each `obj-` line only recurses if its CONFIG_ is set):**

| step | file:line | rule |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `Kbuild:94` | `obj-y += drivers/` (unconditional â€” top-level `Kbuild`, not `Makefile`) |
| 2 | `drivers/Makefile:95` | `obj-y += net/` (unconditional) |
| 3 | `drivers/net/Makefile:52` | `obj-$(CONFIG_ETHERNET) += ethernet/` |
| 4 | `drivers/net/ethernet/Makefile:50` | `obj-$(CONFIG_NET_VENDOR_INTEL) += intel/` |
| 5 | `drivers/net/ethernet/intel/Makefile:8` | `obj-$(CONFIG_E1000E) += e1000e/` |
| 6 | `drivers/net/ethernet/intel/e1000e/Makefile:11` | `obj-$(CONFIG_E1000E) += e1000e.o` (+ objs at lines 13-15 above) |

**Corresponding Kconfig gates that must all evaluate true/`source`d for `CONFIG_E1000E` to even be visible/selectable:**

- `drivers/net/Kconfig:6-9` â€” `menuconfig NETDEVICES`, `depends on NET`, `default y if UML`; body wrapped `if NETDEVICES` (line 27) â€¦ `endif # NETDEVICES` (line 647).
- `drivers/net/Kconfig:490` â€” `source "drivers/net/ethernet/Kconfig"` (inside the `if NETDEVICES` block).
- `drivers/net/ethernet/Kconfig:6-9` â€” `menuconfig ETHERNET`, `depends on NET`, `default y`; body wrapped `if ETHERNET` (line 13) â€¦ `endif # ETHERNET` (line 196).
- `drivers/net/ethernet/Kconfig:86` â€” `source "drivers/net/ethernet/intel/Kconfig"` (inside `if ETHERNET`).
- `drivers/net/ethernet/intel/Kconfig:6-8` â€” `config NET_VENDOR_INTEL`, `bool "Intel devices"`, `default y`; body wrapped `if NET_VENDOR_INTEL` (line 17) â€¦ `endif # NET_VENDOR_INTEL` (line 359).
- `drivers/net/ethernet/intel/Kconfig:58-62` â€” `config E1000E` itself (see (a)).

**Caveat:** this repo checkout is a trimmed subset of the kernel tree â€” there is no top-level `Kconfig`/`drivers/Kconfig` file present (verified: `find . -maxdepth 1 -iname "Kconfig*"` and `find . -iname Kconfig` return nothing above `drivers/net/Kconfig`), so I can't cite the file:line that sources `drivers/net/Kconfig` into the root menu, nor whatever gates `CONFIG_NET` itself. Everything from `drivers/net/Kconfig` downward, and the full Makefile descent from top-level `Kbuild` downward, is verified directly in this checkout.
